name: Shabu Shabu Dinner
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Shabu shabu or sukiyaki meat
12 large prawns (shell on but tail left on)
12 Green onions (trimmed, with part of green left on)
12 oz Firm tofu (cut into 1 inch cubes)
Optional: 1 piece Kombu (kelp), about 3 inches square
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients by slicing the rib-eye or New York steak paper thin.
2. Place an electric wok or skillet in the center of the table.
3. Arrange meat, seafood, vegetables, and tofu on platters.
4. Place cooked rice in a serving bowl and set it on the table.
5. Set out bowls of pickles and serve individual small plates for pickles and dipping sauces at each diner's place.
6. Bring chicken broth and Kombu to a simmer in the wok or skillet.
7. Remove and discard the Kombu if used.
8. Allow diners to select and cook their own meat and vegetables in the broth using chopsticks or forks.
9. At the conclusion of dinner, pour remaining broth into bowls and pass them around the table.
Nutritional Information per serving (excluding pickled vegetables or sauces):
- 515 calories
- 41g protein
- 67g carbohydrates
- 10g fat (3g saturated)
- 89mg cholesterol
- 205mg sodium
- Fiber not specified
Source: Karola Saekel, San Francisco Chronicle, 7/14/93.
